items.text: WorkflowId is a newtype over Uuid deriving Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug, Eq, Hash, and Display.
ActivityId is a newtype whose value derives from the activity's scheduling sequence position, enabling deterministic replay matching.
TimerId is a newtype supporting both author-assigned (named) and engine-assigned (anonymous) construction.
RunId is a newtype distinguishing successive runs of the same logical workflow (reset / continue-as-new).
No public signature in aion-core accepts a bare Uuid or String where a domain identifier is meant.
Event enum has workflow-lifecycle variants: WorkflowStarted, WorkflowCompleted, WorkflowFailed, WorkflowCancelled, WorkflowTimedOut.
Event enum has activity variants: ActivityScheduled, ActivityStarted, ActivityCompleted, ActivityFailed (with attempt), ActivityCancelled.
Event enum has timer variants: TimerStarted, TimerFired, TimerCancelled.
Event enum has a SignalReceived variant carrying signal name and payload.
Event enum has child-workflow variants: ChildWorkflowStarted, ChildWorkflowCompleted, ChildWorkflowFailed, ChildWorkflowCancelled.
Every event carries an envelope with a monotonic per-workflow sequence number, a recorded timestamp, and the owning WorkflowId.
Event derives Serialize and Deserialize and round-trips losslessly through serde_json.
Payload is a newtype over bytes with a content-type tag.
Payload constructs from and converts to serde_json::Value and round-trips losslessly.
Activity inputs/results, signal payloads, and workflow inputs are stored as Payload; Event carries no generic type parameter.
WorkflowStatus enum has variants Running, Completed, Failed, Cancelled, TimedOut.
WorkflowStatus is computed by projecting over event history (the last lifecycle event determines the status).
No code path sets WorkflowStatus independently of the events that justify it.
WorkflowFilter supports filtering by workflow type, status, time range, and parent workflow.
WorkflowSummary carries workflow id, type, status, start time, and end time.
ActivityError carries an explicit retryable-vs-terminal classification, not a string or bool.
WorkflowError is defined as the terminal failure type of a workflow.
StoreError is a closed enum with SequenceConflict, NotFound, Backend, and Serialization variants.
EventStore is an async trait that is Send + Sync + 'static.
append accepts an expected sequence number, applies all events atomically, and returns SequenceConflict if the stored head does not match.
read_history returns the complete event history for a workflow in sequence order.
list_active returns the WorkflowIds of all non-terminal workflows.
query returns WorkflowSummary values matching a WorkflowFilter.
schedule_timer persists a durable timer and expired_timers returns timers due to fire as of a given instant.
InMemoryStore implements EventStore in full, including timer scheduling and retrieval.
InMemoryStore append enforces the expected-sequence guard and applies events atomically (all or none).
A reusable behavioural test suite exercises append/read/list/query/timers plus conflict and atomicity, runnable against any EventStore, and passes against InMemoryStore.
